title: Anthropic crisis over alleged links with China leads to G7 restrictions
summary: The case of the AI company and SK Telecom set off alarms in the US, resulting in limitations on its models and an international debate about access to artificial intelligence.

title: Adobe incorporates AI assistants into Photoshop, Premiere, Illustrator and InDesign
summary: The company deploys intelligent agents in its creative tools to automate tasks and improve productivity.

title: Midjourney unveils AI-powered full-body medical scanner
summary: The generative imaging company showcased an ultrasound device that scans the body in 60 seconds without radiation.

title: US government prioritizes AI data centers in grid connection
summary: New regulations backed by Trump accelerate access to electricity for artificial intelligence data centers.

title: Apple confirms price increases on iPhone and MacBook due to memory shortage
summary: High demand for DRAM, NAND and HBM from AI drives up costs and forces increases on Apple devices.

title: Noam Shazeer leaves Google and returns to OpenAI
summary: The co-leader of Gemini joins OpenAI again, where he previously worked, after two years on Google's AI team.

title: Bertín Osborne founds Española de Telefonía, a 'traditionalist and right-wing' mobile operator
summary: The Spanish artist and presenter launches his own mobile operator with a conservative approach and promises of transparency.

title: SSD prices skyrocket and retail market is on the verge of collapse
summary: Solid state drives reach such high prices that a 2 TB unit costs more than a PS5, while manufacturers report the disappearance of the retail market.

title: China bets on its own memory chips to alleviate global shortage
summary: The Chinese government orders CXMT and YMTC to prioritize manufacturing for consumption, easing the RAM and SSD crisis while Chinese chips emerge as a salvation.

title: OpenAI prepares its IPO and strengthens its management team
summary: The artificial intelligence company brings in high-profile executives on the home stretch to its initial public offering.

title: Waymo recalls nearly 4,000 robotaxis to prevent them from driving in construction zones on highways
summary: The autonomous vehicle company fixes a problem that caused its taxis to enter road work areas.

title: Massive leak in Texas: 3 million driver's licenses and passports stolen
summary: A cyberattack on state government systems compromises personal data of millions of citizens.

title: NASA selects Eric Schmidt's rocket company for a mission to Mars
summary: The space agency chooses the company founded by the former Google CEO to compete with SpaceX in Martian exploration.

title: SpaceX agrees to buy Anysphere, creator of Cursor, for $60 billion
summary: Elon Musk's company acquires the AI-based development tools company in one of the largest deals in the sector.

title: Apple prepares launch of second-generation iPhone Air for spring 2027
summary: Apple's new slim model will hit the market with significant improvements over the first version.

title: GTA 6 reveals official cover art and pre-order date: June 25
summary: Rockstar Games announces the official cover of the long-awaited video game and the date to pre-order it.

title: ChatGPT market share falls below 50% for the first time as Gemini and Claude grow
summary: In May 2026, ChatGPT loses ground to Gemini and Claude, which reach 25% and 10% respectively.

title: Sleep Number files for bankruptcy and will be delisted from Nasdaq
summary: The smart mattress company files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy and its shares plummet.

title: Swiss parliament lifts ban on building new nuclear power plants
summary: Switzerland turns its energy policy around by once again allowing the construction of nuclear plants.

title: Meta's CTO admits morale at the company is 'probably the worst' after layoffs
summary: Layers of layoffs and restructuring have deeply damaged employee morale at Meta.
